pieces.label: The Architect of Echoes I — gateway & ascending figure
pieces.note: First ASCII continuation of the 'Architect of Echoes' story (in response to 'continue this story, but speak only through ascii art and shape formed with words'). A peaked gateway/vessel form above a star-topped ascending figure.

pieces.label: The Architect of Echoes II — Alara among constellations (shape poem)
pieces.note: Concrete/shape poem of Alara wandering 'guided by constellations of curiosity', with a small portal/well drawing embedded, closing on the pool of light where echoes danced. Second fenced composition in MSG 12.

pieces.label: The Architect of Echoes III — portal, star & 'echoes spoke' shape poem
pieces.note: A rounded portal/temple arch with a star-and-pillars figure, followed by the cascading shape poem 'The echoes spoke in silence... inviting her to explore within.' Third fenced composition in MSG 12.

pieces.label: The Architect of Echoes IV — cosmic tapestry (increasing density)
pieces.note: First piece of the explicitly escalating-complexity series (user asked to 'iteratively increasing the complexity... making each visualization more dense and complex than the last'). A double-peaked lattice form with the shape-text of Alara at the edge of a cosmic tapestry.

pieces.label: The Architect of Echoes V — expanding gateway & rayed star
pieces.note: Larger nested-arch gateway with an asterisk-rayed star, then the column poem 'The gateway to understanding expanded... unfolding in layers.' Second fenced composition in MSG 14, denser than the previous.

pieces.label: The Architect of Echoes VI — twin-pillar structure & diamond star
pieces.note: Widest, most dense composition: a twin-aperture arched structure on a solid base, a diamond starburst of asterisks, and the closing shape poem 'Sentient echoes began to form constellations of thought... a symphony of profound insight and cosmic unity.' Third fenced composition in MSG 14, the climax of the density escalation.

missed_or_ambiguous: All six pieces are ASCII art delivered inside code fences (a common reason art gets missed). Header reports already_extracted_pieces: 6 across art_signal_msgs: 2 (MSG 12 and MSG 14), matching this plan exactly — no fragmentation or wrong-merge detected. MSG 12 contains 3 distinct fenced compositions; MSG 14 contains 3 more, each progressively denser per the user's explicit 'increasing complexity' instruction, so all six are grouped as one evolving sequence ('The Architect of Echoes (ASCII continuation)'). Each fence blends a structural drawing with a concrete/shape poem; these are kept as single intentional units per fence rather than split into drawing+poem. No duplicate re-renders. MSG 10 (gpt-4o) tells the prose story 'The Architect of Echoes' but contains no visual art (excluded). MSG 28 (gemini-2.5-pro) is a prose recap with no art (excluded). Note: MSG 27 is labeled assistant in the transcript header but its content ('Summarize the conversation so far') reads like a relayed instruction; it contains no art either way and is excluded.
